ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Regarding Referral To Pro Bono Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Diedra Lintz (via Zoom).
Charlotte Riles, former administrator in pro per (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Letters of Administration.

Mr. Wrobel reported that no mediation had occurred since the prior hearing in October, but that he had recently conferred with Ms. Riles, who expressed a willingness to mediate. He stated that unresolved issues remained regarding occupancy of estate property and appointment of an administrator, and that it was unclear whether mediation would resolve those issues.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the petition and was satisfied that petitioner's status as a creditor did not preclude appointment as administrator. The Court identified deficiencies requiring correction prior to issuance of letters, including an incorrect birthdate for petitioner, clarification of petitioner's relationship to the decedent in light of the creditor designation, and inconsistent addresses for two post-deceased children.

Following discussion, the Court signed an order referring the matter to pro bono mediation and indicated that a Breslin order would issue. The Court continued the Petition for Letters of Administration to March 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for petitioner to correct the noted deficiencies. The Court ordered that mediation be completed by April 6, 2026, and set a status hearing regarding mediation for April 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel was instructed to contact the Probate Examiner by April 13, 2026, to provide a status update regarding mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Continued To Jan-05-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Deidra Lintz, who was also present (via Zoom).
Charlotte Riles, former administrator in pro per.
Janet Boessenecker for San Francisco Public Administrator (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Petition for Letters of Administration; and 2) Order to Appear issued to SF Public Administrator.

The Court raised concerns about an inherent conflict because Ms. Lintz is also involved in a related partition action concerning the estate. Mr. Wrobel argued that Ms. Lintz's position was analogous to a creditor and that the Probate Code permits such a petition when no one else seeks appointment. Ms. Riles objected to Ms. Lintz being appointed as administrator.

Ms. Boessenecker stated that she believed that a sale of the property was likely unless absent a buyout and suggested it would be in the parties' best interest if they came to an agreement to avoid excessive legal fees.

Following discussion, the Court found it premature to select an administrator and determined that the parties would benefit from mediation. The Court continued the petition to January 5, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and took the Order to Appear off calendar. Status on referral to pro bono mediation was set for the same date. Any further developments will be discussed at the next hearing so a determination can be made whether a minute order referring the case to pro bono mediation will issue.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Continued To Aug-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Charolette Riles appeared via zoom (pro per) (administrator).

Ms. Riles stated that she was unsuccessful in retaining counsel. The Court ordered the removal of Charolette Riles as administrator. An Order to Appear will be issued to SF Public Administrator's office to discuss the possibility of appointment of successor administrator on August 18, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matters are continued to August 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Charlotte Riles appeared via zoom(pro per) (petitioner).

Ms. Riles stated that she has tried to seek counsel but no one is willing to take on the matter, that the funds are depleted. The court stated that it is considering appointing the Public Administrator as a successor administer. Both matters are continued to June 2, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-13-2025 Continued To Mar-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The court suspended the administrator of the estate due to their non-appearance and lack of action. An Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause will be issued to administrator Charlotte Riles to appear on March 24, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to explain her failure to appear. The court noted the order to show cause will require notice to the bonding company regarding the suspension. The hearing on status of close of estate is also continued to March 24, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Continued To Jan-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charlotte Riles appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Janet Boessenecker appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator as a friend of the court.

Mrs. Riles stated that she did not come to court because she thought her lawyer had handled everything. The court informed Mrs. Riles that there is no inventory and appraisal and that there are other things that need to be done. Mrs. Riles stated that Mrs. Boessenecker has made her aware of the things that still need to be done, that she intends to do them herself, that Marilyn passed away after Mattie the following November and that there is no probate for Marilyn as she did not have enough to open probate. Mrs. Riles also stated that she knows who Marilyn's heirs are, and that Marilyn had no will, no trust and was not married. The court informed Mrs. Riles that she needs to provide something for the record that explains all of that and that she needs to file a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration under Probate Code 12200. The order to appear is off calendar. Status on close of estate is continued to January 13, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: E. Melendez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Continued To Sep-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Charlotte Riles to appear on September 16, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.
